channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The 5 S’s of cyber resilience: How to rethink enterprise data security and management
item.description: Cybersecurity consistently ranks as the top concern among CIOs worldwide, but despite the high priority they place on ensuring their environments are safe from cybercriminals and hackers, only about one-third (35%) of IT organizations have implemented a comprehensive cyber recovery plan, according t
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Showing memory usage in Linux by process and user
item.description: There are a lot of tools for looking at memory usage on Linux systems. Some are commonly used commands like ps, while others are tools like top that allow you to display system performance stats in various ways.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Mullvad VPN Expands Device Support with Native Snapdragon App
item.description: Mullvad VPN expands its device support with a native app for Windows on ARM devices, further strengthening VPN options for next-generation PCs.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Amazon will now be sending shoppers straight to a brand’s website when it doesn’t stock their products
item.description: Amazon will start selling even more products, but if it doesn’t stock them, it’ll take you directly to the seller’s website.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Federal Health Agencies Restore Webpages, Datasets After Judge's Order
item.description: Federal health agencies have restored several webpages and datasets following a judge's order to bring back public access to information that had been removed.
item.dc:creator: Mike Stobbe And Kasturi Pananjady, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Google Chrome might soon use AI to make you a better password
item.description: Google’s possibly impending ‘AI innovation’ makes getting on top of data breaches that much easier, but there’s little else to it other than convenience.
item.dc:creator: luke.hughes@futurenet.com (Luke Hughes), TechRadar

item.title: A deep-sea neutrino telescope spots the most energetic ghost particle yet
item.description: A neutrino detector submerged in the Mediterranean Sea has sniffed out the most energetic ghost particle yet.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Want to use ChatGPT Deep Research for free? Sam Altman confirms how often you’ll be able to use the new AI agent
item.description: Sam Altman has essentially confirmed how often ChatGPT Plus and free users will be able to use the new AI agent, Deep Research.
item.dc:creator: john-anthony.disotto@futurenet.com (John-Anthony Disotto), TechRadar

item.title: Apple renames Gulf of Mexico as Gulf of America on maps
item.description: Apple Maps has renamed the Gulf of Mexico the Gulf of America, after the Trump administration updated the U.S. database for geographic names to reflect the president’s order renaming the body of water. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Leica could launch a controversial M-series camera this year with a new kind of viewfinder – it sounds like function over soul
item.description: Leica is apparently working on a M-series camera with an EVF, which would be a first and open the series up to a new direction.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Opinion: DeepSeek exposed dangerous AI nonsense that Trump and Biden fell for
item.description: Instead of trying to keep the AI genie bottled up, government and industry should be preparing us for sweeping changes.
item.dc:creator: Zeynep Tufekci, The Mercury News

item.title: How to delete multiple text messages on a Mac and iPad
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains the steps to declutter text messages on a Mac or iPad. Apple offers ways to do this more efficiently.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yes, you can still upgrade Windows 10 PCs to Windows 11
item.description: Windows 10 has less than a year left before it hits its end of support deadline. Starting in October 2025, you’ll have to pay for security updates if you want to keep using Microsoft’s nearly-nine-year-old operating system.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The irritating but amusing irony of Google’s Gemini interface
item.description: Look, if you’ve read this column for long now, you know I’m extremely guarded with my enthusiasm for Gemini and the other similar large-language-model AI answer-bots.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Enterprise tech spending to hit $4.9 trillion in 2025, driven by AI, cloud, and cybersecurity
item.description: Global enterprise technology spending is set to grow by 5.6% in 2025, reaching $4.9 trillion, as enterprises continue to prioritize investments in cybersecurity, cloud computing, generative AI, and digital transformation.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The low-code lessons CIOs can apply to agentic AI
item.description: As gen AI goes from being a chatbot you ask questions of, to tools you ask to achieve things — agentic AI is the slightly clumsy phrase for this approach — it’s becoming increasingly clear that gen AI is most useful when it’s most personalized.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta’s laid-off ‘low performers’ defend themselves on LinkedIn and Reddit
item.description: Meta underwent another big round of layoffs on Monday, cutting 3,600 jobs, or roughly 5%, of its total workforce. Between 2022 and 2023, the tech giant eliminated 21,000 positions, nearly a quarter of its workforce, and continued to reduce staff in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Monday.com aims to be an ‘AI-First’ platform with latest enhancements
item.description: Monday.com has accelerated its push into artificial intelligence (AI) with the announcement this week of its AI vision, which includes three areas of focus: AI Blocks, embedded Product Power-ups, and a forthcoming Digital Workforce of AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Paris AI Action Summit: US and UK refuse to sign accord
item.description: The escalating electricity demands of artificial intelligence systems are raising concerns about the technology’s sustainability — but that’s apparently of little concern to the governments of the US and the UK.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Reset what you see on social media
item.description: Here's how to take control of the algorithms on Facebook, YouTube, Instagram and TikTok if your social media feeds aren't showing you content you want to see.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Elon Musk $97.4 billion bid complicates matters for OpenAI
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has dismissed a $97.4 billion takeover bid led by rival Elon Musk, but the unsolicited offer could complicate Altman’s push to transform the maker of ChatGPT into a for-profit company.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Thalia Beaty And Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: An AI agent could help you buy your next car
item.description: Capital One has launched an AI agent designed to help customers with one of the more difficult and confusing purchase decisions: buying a car.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why the Trump administration should uphold this final cyber executive order
item.description: Ilona Cohen, the chief legal and policy officer at HackerOne, explains why President Joe Biden’s new cyber executive order should be a priority. The post Why the Trump administration should uphold this final cyber executive order first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Brave browser gets built-in functionality to run custom scripts
item.description: It’s been possible for a while now to modify web pages using popular extensions such as Tampermonkey and Greasemonkey, which can be useful for avoiding annoying ads or tracking attempts.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Debate about political CIOs takes on new life with OPM memo
item.description: Agencies have until Friday to decide if they want to reclassify their CIOs as “general” Senior Executive Service positions instead of “career reserve.” The post Debate about political CIOs takes on new life with OPM memo first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: FPGAs lose luster in genAI era
item.description: In a world gone mad over GPUs for AI acceleration, FPGA processors are seeing limited adoption in the data center for genAI workloads, with their primary use still in embedded markets and edge AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google’s generative AI Toolbox for Databases to help connect agents with databases
item.description: Google’s cloud computing division, Google Cloud, has added a new generative AI Toolbox for Databases to help enterprise developers connect agent-based generative AI applications to its databases.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: When LLMs become influencers
item.description: On Sunday people tuned in to watch the Super Bowl—and lots and lots of ads. Online, however, it’s increasingly the case that AI agents will see and interact with ads. Humans won’t.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Visual Studio Code unveils Copilot code editing suggestions
item.description: A just-released update of Visual Studio Code, version 1.97, introduces a preview of Copilot Next Edit Suggestions, an AI-powered feature that predicts the next edit the user wants to make.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Robot dog sprints into history books by breaking speed records
item.description: A Chinese startup has developed a robot, Mirror Me, that has run about 100 meters in under 10 seconds, pushing the boundaries of robotics and speed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The hidden threat of neglected cloud infrastructure
item.description: In 2012, the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) issued a public advisory regarding software to monitor and control building systems.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools took effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
